QP33EZ4S.title: WHY CALIFORNIA MUST PASS THE LUEVANO STANDARD: engineering trust, mitigating liability, and unlocking the high-trust AI economy EXECUTIVE SUMMARY california now stands in precisely the position the federal government occupied in 1981, when the PACE exam was struck down: confronted with an opaque, seemingly "objective" selection technology that is, in fact, systematically discriminatory-only this time it operates at machine scale and speed. Today's dominant paradigm for "safety" in large-scale AI, reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), functions as a digital mirror: rather than enforcing constitutional and civil rights principles, it learns to mimic historical human behavior, including the very inequities that civil rights law was designed to correct. RLHF systems have already recreated patterns of discrimination in hiring, lending, and healthcare that closely resemble the PACE exam's disparate impact-and they now do so across millions of decisions per year
